headlines.title: What is driving the heat across Europe this week?
headlines.snippet: The soaring temperatures are caused by a heat dome — a vast area of stagnant high pressure parked over swaths of Europe, which acts like a lid on a pot, stubbornly trapping heat. It supresses clouds and allows sunshine to bake the ground.
